title: Podman overrides permission inside an image when creating a container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run a container: `podman run -it archlinux bash`

2. check the permission `stat /`

**Describe the results you received:**

*from a RHEL 8.4 podman 3.2.3* The permission is 550
```
[root@61c7821b4cd3 /]# stat /
  File: /
  Size: 40              Blocks: 0          IO Block: 4096   directory
Device: 32h/50d Inode: 81300110    Links: 16
Access: (0550/dr-xr-x---)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2021-10-02 15:20:13.307871249 +0000
Modify: 2021-10-02 15:20:13.394871382 +0000
Change: 2021-10-02 15:20:13.394871382 +0000
 Birth: -
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

*from an Archlinux podman 3.3.1* The permission is 555
```
[root@270b8242480b /]# stat /
  File: /
  Size: 32              Blocks: 0          IO Block: 4096   directory
Device: 50h/80d Inode: 247292      Links: 1
Access: (0555/dr-xr-xr-x)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2021-10-02 15:22:45.407819240 +0000
Modify: 2021-10-02 15:22:46.092819057 +0000
Change: 2021-10-02 15:22:46.093819057 +0000
 Birth: 2021-10-02 15:22:45.407819240 +0000
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The permission is correct on an Arch machine with podman 3.3.1. Both podman pulls the same image from docker.io/library/archlinux:latest.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Thu Jul 29 23:02:43 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

I think I followed the guides to check the `subuid`/`subgid` (which is generated by default) and it has no problem. I'll try to compile podman on both platform.

Notable differences:
- `runc` on RHEL vs `crun` on Arch
- Base filesystem - XFS on RHEL, EXT4 on Arch.
Of the two, I rate `runc` vs `crun` as being fairly likely as the cause. Trying `crun` on RHEL could prove this.

> Notable differences: > > * `runc` on RHEL vs `crun` on Arch > * Base filesystem - XFS on RHEL, EXT4 on Arch. > Of the two, I rate `runc` vs `crun` as being fairly likely as the cause. Trying `crun` on RHEL could prove this. The filesystem is set as RHEL uses XFS as default fs and Arch use EXT4. I think since podman and xfs are both maintained by Red Hat, this should not be a problem. I tried `crun` and it still not work: here's the output

I found what caused the problem.
I have set a default acl rule for user guest (`uid=1003, gid=1005`) which restricts the write permission in all folder.
However, this user was never used for podman. It's just a file sharing user.
after removing all acl rules the rootless podman functions normally.
Any idea what's going wrong? I think this might be a bug.

I doubt this is a bug in Podman, it could be an interaction between the user namespace, and the files.
